title: poa alopecurus subsp. fuegiana
intro: Poa alopecurus subsp. fuegiana, also known as Fuegian meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the southern Andes. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Poa alopecurus subsp. fuegiana include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the plant material in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
